Document:
# Core Rule 2 - Metagaming and External Information
Wonder Cluster allows game knowledge and social continuity between rounds. Players may understand certain antagonist mechanics, common tactics, and maintain persistent character relationships. However, players should not use information gained outside of in-character means to gain an unfair advantage or unintended advantage during a round.
## What Is Allowed
- General knowledge of game mechanics and certain antagonist roles - Persistent friendships, rivalries, or character history - Playing with friends, provided actions remain roleplay-driven - Out-of-character communication that does not influence round outcomes
## What Is Not Allowed
- Relaying antagonist locations or objectives - Coordinating attacks, fights, or through external channels - Watching streams to gain round information - Acting on knowledge of hidden roles (e.g., Nuclear Operatives, traitors, chameleon users) without in-character discovery
Secrecy-based roles and mechanics rely on uncertainty to function. Preemptively countering them using outside information undermines fair play.
